signals.reasoning: This has escalated from a cleanup exit into an emergency cover. The live SOL short is now down about 350 dollars after previously being up about 107, retained none of peak PnL, still has no stop, and liquidation distance has collapsed to roughly 0.61%. The short thesis is broken and the remaining risk is unacceptable.

BTC is now decisively stronger on the day and that strength is exactly why the SOL short failed. In this position-management context, the correct action is to flatten the open short rather than force a new BTC view.
ETH is also breaking higher with BTC, which further weakens the case for defending the SOL short, but it is not a better immediate action than covering the emergency risk.
BOME is just another high-beta rebound name and does not matter more right now than closing the failing SOL position.
FARTCOIN remains constructive, which only reinforces that the market is not in the downside regime needed to defend the SOL short.
MERL is already far into rebound territory and is not a better immediate decision than getting out of the emergency SOL risk.
PNUT is recovering, but it is too secondary and too thin to matter in a board dominated by an urgent bad position.
SPX has trend support, but position management here is about removing dangerous open risk first, not rotating into another high-beta name.
TST is too extended and too crowded to be relevant while the SOL short is approaching emergency territory.
KNEIRO has incomplete data and is not where disciplined attention should go in an emergency cover situation.
